metadata.title: Video to Prompt
metadata.description: Turn any video into structured prompts for AI video generation. Extract scene, style, camera language, and shot list in seconds.
metadata.keywords: video to prompt, AI prompt generator, video prompt builder, shot list generator, prompt engineering, AI video workflow

hero-tool.title: Turn Raw Video Into Production-Ready Prompts
hero-tool.description: Upload one reference clip and get a reusable prompt package with scene summary, master prompt, short prompt, negative prompt, and shot list.
hero-tool.description2: Analyze visual language, camera movement, subject action, lighting, and composition — then copy a prompt package your team can actually reuse.
hero-tool.tagline: Video to Prompt · Upload once, decompose once, reuse everywhere
hero-tool.hint: Built for prompt engineering, reenactment planning, and AI video production workflows.

features.title: What You Actually Get From One Video
features.description: Instead of vague inspiration, you get structured assets your team can copy into real generation and production workflows.

items.title: Scene Summary
items.description: Condense the clip into a clear scene brief before anyone starts prompting.

items.title: Master Prompt
items.description: Generate a detailed baseline prompt that captures visual intent and execution logic.

items.title: Short Prompt
items.description: Get a compressed prompt version for faster iteration and lightweight experiments.

items.title: Negative Prompt
items.description: Reduce drift by explicitly listing what should not appear in the generated output.

items.title: Keyword Stack
items.description: Extract the visual vocabulary — style, mood, lighting, materials, pacing, and subject cues.

items.title: Shot List
items.description: Break the clip into actionable camera and scene directions you can recreate or adapt.

use-cases.title: Where Video to Prompt Fits Best
use-cases.description: Map the workflow to four common YouTube production scenarios and reuse the same prompt system.

items.title: Shorts Hook Breakdown
items.description: Decompose high-retention hook clips into pacing, framing, and promptable action cues.

items.title: Tutorial Segment Rebuild
items.description: Turn instructional clips into reusable prompt structures for repeatable talk-through scenes.

items.title: Review Shot Planning
items.description: Extract close-ups, transitions, and narrative beats into a clean shot-by-shot prompt pack.

items.title: Vlog Sequence Library
items.description: Convert daily footage into searchable prompt assets your team can reuse across episodes.

usage.label: 3-step workflow
usage.title: How the Workflow Runs
usage.description: From reference video to reusable prompt pack, keep the flow to three necessary moves.

items.title: Upload Reference Video
items.description: Choose a clip that already contains the framing, pace, and visual language you want.

items.title: Analyze Shot Structure
items.description: Get scene logic, keywords, master prompt, and shot-by-shot instructions.

items.title: Copy Prompt Package
items.description: Move the output straight into reenactment, prompting, or your internal prompt library.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions About Video to Prompt
faq.description: Questions about prompt packages, supported use cases, and how the workflow fits real production teams.

items.question: What does Video to Prompt generate from one clip?
items.answer: It turns one reference video into a structured prompt package that typically includes a scene summary, master prompt, short prompt, negative prompt, keyword stack, and shot list.

items.question: Is this for AI video generation or for editing existing footage?
items.answer: It is primarily for prompt preparation. The goal is to study a reference clip and convert it into reusable prompt assets that support generation, reenactment, and pre-production planning.

items.question: Who is this product for?
items.answer: It is useful for creators, prompt engineers, ad teams, short-form operators, and production teams who repeatedly translate reference videos into prompt systems.

items.question: Do I need a local GPU or editing pipeline?
items.answer: No. The workflow is cloud-based. You upload the clip, let the system analyze the visual language, and then copy the resulting prompt package.

items.question: Does it return one prompt or multiple layers?
items.answer: Multiple layers. The product is designed around prompt packages, not one-line prompts, so teams can preserve creative intent and reuse it across iterations.

items.question: Can I use this for imitation shooting and shot planning?
items.answer: Yes. That is one of the most practical use cases. The shot list and keyword stack make it easier to translate a reference clip into reproducible direction for filming or generation.

items.question: Can I build a reusable internal prompt library with it?
items.answer: Yes. A strong use case is converting incoming reference videos into searchable prompt assets for teams, clients, or repeated production workflows.

items.question: How do I get access?
items.answer: Use the homepage tool or join the waitlist. We are using early access to refine the workflow around real prompt engineering use cases.

faq.tip: Still have questions? Contact support.

cta.title: Ready to Turn Reference Videos Into Prompt Packages?
